aliases: Peach Oolong Yogurt with Purple Rice
Oolong Peach Purple Rice Yogurt Drink

english: Peach Oolong Yogurt with Purple Rice
Oolong Peach Purple Rice Yogurt Drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for peach oolong purple rice yogurt drink; mapped to closest yogurt-based beverage class. Composite beverage combining oolong tea, Greek yogurt, purple rice, and peach.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (Yoghurt drink) is the closest defensible anchor. No specific code exists for oolong-tea-based yogurt drinks with purple rice in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this composite beverage type; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 350 g (one full drink cup), providing around 574 kcal, 22.8 g protein, 14.7 g fat, and 88.2 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Peach Oolong Purple Rice Yogurt — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database (Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for this dish (cr_id G00010446). All per-100g values are taken as reported. Saturated fat not reported in the HPB record; estimated from Greek yogurt and purple rice composition using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries (USDA #01256 plain Greek yogurt, USDA #20037 brown/purple rice cooked) as a supplementary cross-check, yielding approximately 1.4 g saturated fat per 100 g. Monounsaturated, polyunsaturated, trans fat, and water data not available in the HPB record and set to -1.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Purple Rice and Yogurt-Based Beverages — analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for cooked purple/black rice (GI ~42–50) and plain yogurt (GI ~36), weighted by approximate carbohydrate contribution in this composite drink
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Peach Oolong Purple Rice Yogurt. GI estimated at 55 (low) using the University of Sydney GI Database: purple/black rice cooked GI ~42–50 (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care); plain yogurt GI ~36; oolong tea has negligible carbohydrate contribution. Weighted blended GI calculated from approximate carbohydrate proportions (purple rice ~80% of carbohydrate load, yogurt ~20%). Glycemic load per 350 g serving = (55 × 25.19 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 3.5) / 100 = 12.2 g. This is a conservative low-GI estimate; actual GI may be lower due to resistant starch in purple rice and yogurt protein-fat matrix slowing gastric emptying. A GI of 55 falls within the 'low' category (GI <= 55).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 231 mg/100 g partially offset by potassium 327 mg/100 g (Na:K ratio ~0.71)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit serving size to 200–250 g to keep sodium intake below 460–580 mg per serving; the favourable potassium-to-sodium ratio and oolong tea polyphenols provide some vascular benefit, but total sodium load must be monitored within the daily 2000 mg target.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 25.2 g/100 g with low estimated GI (~55) and dietary fibre 3.7 g/100 g
type2_diabetes.guidance: A 150 g portion provides approximately 37.8 g carbohydrate and 5.6 g fibre; the fibre content and oolong tea polyphenols (theaflavins, catechins) support post-meal glucose moderation, but portion control is essential and the peach component should be confirmed as unsweetened puree rather than syrup to maintain the low reported sugar of 0.21 g/100 g.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (~1.4 g/100 g), zero cholesterol, high potassium (327 mg/100 g), and oolong tea polyphenols
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of low saturated fat, negligible cholesterol, high dietary fibre (3.7 g/100 g), and oolong tea polyphenols with evidence for LDL reduction and endothelial protection makes this a low cardiovascular concern food when consumed in standard 300–350 g portions; sodium at 231 mg/100 g should be factored into daily cardiovascular sodium budgets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Oolong tea polyphenols, purple rice anthocyanins, and yogurt live cultures with anti-inflammatory and gut-immune axis properties
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Oolong tea theaflavins and catechins, purple rice anthocyanins, and yogurt probiotic cultures collectively support reduction of systemic inflammation relevant to RA; ensure the yogurt component contains live active cultures and that peach flavouring is from natural sources to avoid pro-inflammatory artificial additives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Peach Oolong Purple Rice Yogurt is a Singaporean specialty beverage that combines brewed oolong tea with premium Greek yogurt and freshly cooked purple (black glutinous) rice, finished with peach puree or peach flavouring. The drink is thick, creamy, and mildly tangy from the yogurt, with earthy floral notes from the oolong tea, natural sweetness from the peach component, and a chewy textural contrast from the purple rice grains. Per 100 g it provides 164 kcal, 6.5 g protein, 4.21 g fat, 25.19 g carbohydrate, 3.7 g dietary fibre, and 231 mg sodium. Potassium is notably high at 327 mg/100 g and iron at 2.55 mg/100 g, reflecting the mineral density of purple rice. Sugar is very low at 0.21 g/100 g, indicating minimal added sugar. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low) based on the purple rice and yogurt carbohydrate matrix. Clinically, the high fibre, oolong polyphenols, and low sugar make it suitable for type 2 diabetes management with portion control; the potassium-to-sodium ratio is favourable for hypertension; and the low saturated fat and anti-inflammatory phytonutrients (anthocyanins from purple rice, catechins from oolong tea) support cardiovascular and rheumatoid arthritis management. This drink is vegetarian, halal-friendly, and gluten-free. It is popular as a meal-replacement or snack beverage in Singapore bubble tea and specialty drink culture.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010446, confidence 0.95 per source record). Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data for Greek yogurt and purple rice as a supplementary cross-check. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database entries for purple rice and yogurt; no direct GI measurement exists for this composite drink.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (1.4 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for plain Greek yogurt (USDA #01256) and cooked purple/brown rice (USDA #20037) as the HPB record does not report saturated fat separately. This value should be confirmed against the product label or a direct laboratory analysis. 2. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions and moisture data for this composite beverage are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. Cholesterol is reported as 0.0 mg/100 g in the HPB source record; this is plausible given the low-fat yogurt and plant-based rice components and is treated as confirmed zero per the HPB source. 4. GI of 55 is a conservative analogous estimate; actual GI may be lower due to resistant starch in purple rice and the protein-fat matrix of Greek yogurt slowing gastric emptying. A direct GI measurement study would improve confidence. 5. The peach component is assumed to be unsweetened puree based on the very low reported sugar (0.21 g/100 g); if sweetened syrup is used, sugar and carbohydrate values would be materially higher. 6. Vitamin and micronutrient fields not reported by HPB (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1 through b12, folate, vitamin_d, vitamin_e, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg) are set to -1 as data is unavailable in the primary source.
